channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: After Going Quiet For Half A Year, Halo: Infinite Surprise Drops New Firefight Gauntlet
item.content:encoded:
Everyone swore Halo: Infinite was dead… But a new mode has suddenly dropped. 

They did, indeed, claim that they had the “last planned multiplayer update” in their November 2025 gamaplay update—even my real-life friend who I normally see at the club mourned it a bit.

It turns out that the word “planned” might have been doing a lot of lifting. 

Now, Halo Studios has dropped “Firefight: Gauntlet,” a continuation of the popular PvE mode that lets players take on waves of increasingly enemies while taking on objectives throughout the maps. Players navigate five maps connected by a resupply and customization zone, allowing players to navigate back and prepare for what’s next. At the end of each round, they’ll also encounter a big boss to take down. 

“This deviously difficult PvE mode introduces upgradeable attributes, elimination objectives, and auto-scaled difficulty settings to a boss-filled Firefight experience designed to challenge even the toughest Spartans,” the official blog on the new mode reads. 

This Halo: Infinite update is out now, though that seems to be all there is to the update.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/after-going-quiet-for-half-a-year-halo-infinite-surprise-drops-new-firefight-gauntlet-2000137998
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Mortal Online 2's Newest Features Are Up, With New Journal, Party System, And Status Bars
item.content:encoded:
The latest Mortal Online 2 patch has made it out of the Public Test Realm and goes live today, heavily updating the Journal and status bars while adding a fresh party feature. 

The first of the heavy-hitting changes is the addition of the new party system, which lets up to five friends and strangers alike team up for temporary communications perks, and hopefully encourages solo players to try things out with others. 

As part of this system, other party members are marked as Friendly, and they get shared Glory upon kills. Players can chat through a new text channel and invite players in a multitude of other ways; players are kicked upon leaving the world (and through a timeout after a disconnect), and they’ll disband as well when the server goes down. 

Another huge update is the new Journal, which sees a heavy overhaul that shows off new stats and a new Title system. Basically, they’ve moved all of your accomplishments here, started tracking 20 new ones, and have pushed them together. Worth noting in the meantime, though, is that your Title needs to be re-selected since the tab got removed from the character sheet and moved here. Also, Discoveries have been moved into the Journal, and Foraging and Fishing Discovery groups have been made, letting you truly track everything in one place. 

Status bars have also gotten rebuilt from the ground up, including character and pet health, mana, and more. A ton of the changes are for ease of visibility, including tweaks to when certain ones are shown, like hiding non-damageable targets’ bars, and only showing stamina if a pet is mountable.

The patch comes with a ton of other fixes as well around the game, as usual. And down the road, we’re still waiting for a big new continent.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/mortal-online-2s-newest-features-are-up-with-new-journal-party-system-and-status-bars-2000137999
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen's Combat & Progression Update PTR Goes Live Today
item.content:encoded:
Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen’s PTR for its big Combat and Progression update will go live today. Visionary Realms hasn’t said just when the server will be up just yet, but the team did release details and a FAQ if you’re looking to test the huge, game-changing update.

The team is set to make an announcement when it all goes live, but until then we have an FAQ as well as a description to let us know what to expect. Visionary Realms recently announced the PTR was coming as well as some of the content that will and won't be in this first build, when we can expect some updates, and what they hope to get feedback on. 

In the just-released PTR FAQ, we do find out some new info. First, the PTR will be single shard, and this what we can expect going forward, with a single-shard, scaling approach. This is a PTR, so expect changes, wipes, and plenty of things to be broken or not feel right. 

That’s where the feedback comes in, of course. Of course, they want  feedback on any part of the PTR build, but they did release some questions that they hope their testers will remember and give specific feedback on so they can fine-tune those systems.  Mostly though, especially at the beginning, they're looking for organic  testing, so just get in there and play. There might be focused tests or other more specific things they need help with later but we can expect announcements for any of those.

If you purchased the game via the official site, there is a PTR launcher to download. if you bought it through Steam, the same client will be published later and they will update when it's live.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/pantheon-rise-of-the-fallens-combat-and-progression-update-ptr-goes-live-today-2000137997
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Diablo 4's Secret Cow Level Has Been Discovered--But Can Only Be Found Once A Week
item.content:encoded:
The Secret Cow Level is real. No, seriously—players in Diablo 4 have found this title’s version of the long-running franchise’s fan-favorite joke, which started as an “uncle at Nintendo” type of rumor and was soon not only accepted, but also integrated by Blizzard. 

One of the first to document the path to the cow level is LoatheBurger, who explains in a full guide how you can accomplish the Cow Level Quests and find the Cow Island through locations and hints given in the new expansion, Lord of Hatred. 

By rounding up certain weapons, you get a Torn Page that rewards you with a “Moo” title and even a “Moo” emote, as well as a hint to a new location off a coast. As you travel, a ridiculous prophecy tells you about the “Eternal Grazing,” and how, amidst the fires of the downfall of a land, a king is free from his harness from a “realm that did not exist.” 

Of course, when you get there, you find a sketchy cellar and a bunch of human skeletons staring at a bovine statue, sitting around in a “Waiting Room.” Cheeky. 

It turns out there’s one more twist: It has to be Tuesday for the dungeon to unlock. No, seriously. When players went back to actually try the dungeon on Tuesday (4:00 p.m. PDT on Monday, specifically) they could finally kill the cows—and enter the portal that would take them to their bovine chaos. Maybe it's a "Moosday" joke. 

It’s more of an elaborate gag than anything, but it’s a good way to take a break from the chaos of the rest of the Diablo 4 expansion, grinding out its new Warlock class, and all of its Butcher-y fun.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/diablo-4s-secret-cow-level-has-been-discovered-but-can-only-be-found-once-a-week-2000137996
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Temtem: Pioneers Adding New Color Variations, Crema Details Gathering and Resources (and How Tems Will Help)
item.content:encoded:
Crema’s latest Temtem: Pioneers development updates reveal new color variations from meeting a new stretch goal, and lots about resources and crafting in the Downbelow.

With this last reveal, they continue giving an overview of the core gameplay loop. Since this is a survival crafting action RPG, the crafting and the survival go hand in hand and are vital. And you can't do any of that without resources.

Capturing Temtems will require resources to craft TemCards. Want a mount? You’ll have to craft a saddle. Make some trinkets? Need resources. These survival crafting mechanics should be familiar to anyone who knows the genre and the blog does a good job linking the world of Temtem with them.

First, "resources" refers to a variety of things like wood, ore, crystals, berries, mushrooms, and more. Some resources you’ll simply pick up, others will drop from various elements, like a bush giving you both fiber and berries, and others will require tools. You’ll start with basic tools but the tools will require resources to improve them. 

For resources that require tools, there is a minigame associated with them you can choose to play for a potential multiplier on your yield. Or choose to just farm them. Gathering resources that need tools will go directly into your inventory, and your Temtems will help you to gather, speeding up the process a bit (and looking cute while at it).

As of their last stretch goal hit, Crema will be adding new color variations, Auri and Iris. These add an iridescent and a golden option in addition to the Lumas and Umbras, light and dark, color variations.

The Kickstarter has hit $494,297 at this writing, with at least one more promised reveal coming. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/temtem-pioneers-adding-new-color-variations-crema-details-gathering-and-resources-and-how-tems-will-help-2000137995
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: MMOFPS Reaper Actual Heads into Early Access Later This Month
item.content:encoded:
Open-world MMOFPS Reaper Actual will launch into Early Access later this month. Distinct Possibility Studios announced Early Access details, along with pricing tiers and perks.

Early Access will open on May 20thon Steam and there will be three different prices that include access to the game and its world, Marova. Paying for one of the packages also gets you into playtests with the developers and other players, as well as other perks as  the development process continues.

The Foundation Alpha Edition will cost $19.99 and include one Reaper (the game’s name for a playable hero), Havoc, and a bunker base. Next option is Charon’s Pact Edition, Includes all of the above plus two Reapers, Havoc and Luna, and a bunker base at one of seven locations. That will cost $49.99. 

Finally, for $79.99, the Stygian Oath edition gets you all of those things, plus a warehouse base. You’ll also get all four Series 1 Reapers. Those are Havoc, Luna, Thor, and Vibora. Find out more about all four of those characters here on the game info page on the official site.

If you haven't familiarized yourself with the game yet, Reaper Actual will feature an open world, with full progression, crafting, and faction control that can change in real time. Craft within and defend your base, which is vulnerable to raids by other players and NPC factions. 

Then there's the action. This is an FPS so you'll rely on Precision gum play, acquiring new and better gear and weapons, and of course a little dose of squad coordination to help you progress and get through every battle. You'll be able to unlock and craft vehicles for land, sea, and air.

Along with all of the action, the player-driven economy, and the consistent fight over control, promises narrative arcs with progression via the game's mission system that can connect in sometimes surprising ways. Maybe you get a mission to capture an enemy leader and others get sent on a mission to defend that leader. It's one way to stoke tensions and encourage skirmishes. It also offers something different rather than have everything play out exactly the same for all.

Find out lots more over on Steam.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/mmofps-reaper-actual-heads-into-early-access-later-this-month-2000137993
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
